children.name: Ptilonorhynchus
children.description: The satin bowerbird — a monotypic genus of medium-sized bowerbirds. Males are glossy blue-black, while females are greenish-brown. The species is famous for the male's construction of an elaborate avenue-shaped bower of twigs, decorated with blue objects — bottle caps, feathers, berries — to attract females.

children.name: Ptilonorhynchus violaceus
children.description: The classic bowerbird of eastern Australia. Males are glossy violet-blue, females greenish with scalloped underparts. The male builds a walled avenue of twigs, painting the interior with a mixture of saliva and berry juice, then decorates it with blue objects. Immature males are green like females and acquire full adult plumage at age seven.

children.name: Sericulus
children.description: Regent bowerbirds — medium-sized, brightly coloured bowerbirds with striking yellow-and-black plumage. Males build an avenue bower decorated with colourful objects. Found in the forests of eastern Australia and New Guinea.

children.name: Sericulus chrysocephalus
children.description: A stunning bowerbird of eastern Australian rainforests. Brilliant golden-yellow crown and nape contrasting with velvety black body. Builds an avenue bower decorated with yellow leaves, flowers, and berries. Classified as Vulnerable due to habitat loss.

children.name: Sericulus aureus
children.description: A bowerbird with golden-yellow head and black body from New Guinea.

children.name: Sericulus bakeri
children.description: A bowerbird with fiery orange mane from the mountains of New Guinea.

children.name: Chlamydera
children.description: Large grey-brown bowerbirds of Australia and southern New Guinea. They construct the most elaborate avenue bowers of all, decorating them with both natural and human-made objects. Some species paint their bowers with green plant pulp.

children.name: Chlamydera maculata
children.description: Found in inland eastern Australia. Grey-brown with heavy dark spotting above and pink nape patch. Builds a large avenue bower decorated with white objects including bones, stones, and human items. The bower can be over a metre long.

children.name: Chlamydera nuchalis
children.description: The largest bowerbird, found across northern Australia. Sandy-brown with a pink nape patch. Builds an enormous two-walled avenue bower decorated with hundreds of objects, with a preference for white and green items.

children.name: Chlamydera cerviniventris
children.description: A bowerbird from New Guinea and Cape York.

children.name: Chlamydera lauterbachi
children.description: Chlamydera lauterbachi (Yellow-breasted Bowerbird) is a bowerbirds species found in Asia region.

children.name: Chlamydera guttata
children.description: A bowerbird from inland Western Australia.

children.name: Amblyornis
children.description: Bowerbirds that construct maypole bowers from New Guinea.

children.name: Amblyornis inornata
children.description: A bowerbird that builds the most elaborate maypole bower in New Guinea.

children.name: Amblyornis macgregoriae
children.description: A bowerbird with golden crest from the highlands of New Guinea.

children.name: Amblyornis subalaris
children.description: A streaked bowerbird from the mountains of New Guinea.

children.name: Amblyornis flavifrons
children.description: A bowerbird with golden forehead from the Foja Mountains.

children.name: Archboldia
children.description: A monotypic genus of bowerbird from New Guinea.

children.name: Archboldia papuensis
children.description: A large grey bowerbird from the highlands of New Guinea.

children.name: Prionodura
children.description: A monotypic genus of bowerbird from Queensland.

children.name: Prionodura newtoniana
children.description: A bowerbird with golden-yellow plumage from the rainforests of northeastern Queensland.

children.name: Ailuroedus
children.description: Bowerbirds named for their cat-like calls from Australia and New Guinea.

children.name: Ailuroedus crassirostris
children.description: A bright green bird with white spots from eastern Australia.

children.name: Ailuroedus melanotis
children.description: A green catbird from New Guinea and Cape York.

children.name: Ailuroedus buccoides
children.description: A catbird from the lowland forests of New Guinea.

children.name: Ailuroedus geislerorum
children.description: Ailuroedus geislerorum (Geisler's Catbird) is a bowerbirds species found in Asia region.

children.name: Ailuroedus astrictus
children.description: A catbird from the Arfak Mountains of New Guinea.

children.name: Scenopoeetes
children.description: A monotypic genus that clears a display court instead of building a bower.

children.name: Scenopoeetes dentirostris
children.description: A bowerbird with a tooth-like bill serration from the rainforests of northeastern Queensland.

children.name: Ailuroedus
children.description: Catbirds from Australia and New Guinea.

children.name: Ailuroedus crassirostris
children.description: A green catbird from eastern Australia.

children.name: Ailuroedus melanotis
children.description: A catbird from New Guinea and Cape York.

children.name: Ailuroedus buccoides
children.description: Ailuroedus buccoides (White-eared Catbird) is a bowerbirds species found in Asia region.

children.name: Ailuroedus geislerorum
children.description: Ailuroedus geislerorum (Geisler's Catbird) is a bowerbirds species found in Asia region.

children.name: Ailuroedus astrictus
children.description: A catbird from the Arfak Mountains.

children.name: Scenopoeetes
children.description: A bowerbird from Queensland.

children.name: Scenopoeetes dentirostris
children.description: A bowerbird with tooth-like bill from Queensland.
